title: [crash_info.c:18] crash_info_store has no mutex protection
description:
Contract: crash_info_store should safely record crash info from any context.
Violation: The function writes to static crash_info struct without synchronization. If two threads call crash_info_store() concurrently (or if called from ISR while main thread is accessing), the struct could have torn writes (e.g., reason from one call, location from another).
Impact: Low - crash_info is typically called in critical error paths where concurrent calls are unlikely. However, the count increment (line 24) is particularly susceptible to lost updates.
Mitigation: Use atomic operations or accept that crash info may be imprecise under concurrent failure scenarios (reasonable for diagnostic data).
close_reason: Added DESIGN comment explaining intentional lack of mutex: called from error paths where mutex may be corrupted or scheduler broken; 32-bit writes are atomic on Cortex-M; torn writes during concurrent crashes are acceptable since we still capture crash evidence.
